comments.text: This conjecture has a prize of 100$ attached to it as reported by T. Erdelyi in [Er06, p.146].

I performed a Gemini Deep Research query for related literature and it brought up this paper by el Abdalaoui [El25] which seems to answer this problem in the affirmative by proving that there does not exist an ultraflat polynomial with coefficients $\pm1$. [El25] cites [Od18], where Odlyzko made progress on this problem via computational methods. Oddly enough, el Abdalaoui also cites problem 22 in [Er57] as the problem they are providing an answer to, but that problem reads like [230]. Maybe someone with more expertise can take a look at this literature and confirm or deny my understanding.

There are, I believe, multiple serious issues with this paper (I remember discussing with others when it appeared). For example, the final step of the proof on page 9 does not contradict Lemma 5, since the latter is just about the existence of one function with concentration, rather than proving concentration for all functions. I can't recall what the other problems were, but it seemed overall unfixable.

ChatGPT Pro identifies multiple issues with this preprint. Another cause for concern is that the paper relies heavily on a sequence of the author's preprints dating as far back as 2014, almost all of which remain unpublished. I would assess the claims of this preprint as unconfirmed until the preprint is published (or if there is another source of verification, e.g., by an independent human expert, or with a Lean formalization).
